title: Linear-Time String Search and Prefix Structure · Counterexample Repair Bay
summary: Scan large logs for many repeated patterns without repeated restart work. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a verified prefix-based matching engine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute prefix-function fallback lengths while preserving matched-prefix meaning.
Use prefix evidence to avoid rechecking characters in Knuth-Morris-Pratt search.
Use rolling hashes with explicit modulus, collision verification, and numeric limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
